rulings.Case Title: PRISCILLA LAM et al VS. CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 2, 2012, Line 1, DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Granted. Moving party through its evidence including the declarations of Wachtel and Menkes has carried its burden of demonstrating no triable issue of fact on the matter of causation and that no applicable standard of care was violated. It is not disputed that a doctor, not employed by defendant, placed the bottle on the patient's leg. Turning to plaintiff's evidence, I note that the declaration of R. Durham is not admissible for the opinions presented at 7, 19-22 (no basis to believe witness has the expertise; opinions on legal issues [that a certain act breached a duty]). The opinions do not state what policies ought to have been in place, and accordingly cannot support a claim that the lack of policies were a substantial factor in the injury claimed. Indeed, the declaration does not suggest that had certain undefined policies been in place the injuries would not have occurred. The declaration and its appended materials, such as the résumé, do not suggest the witness has relevant expertise or experience on the policies of the type at issue in this case. No basis for the conclusions (that the hospital ought to have had some sort of policies in place) are provided in the declaration. Objections to the Declarations of Durham are sustained. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: SHERAE DRAKE et al VS. JOHN STEWART COMPANY, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs' Motion For Class Certificaton
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 2, 2012, Line 13, Plaintiffs' Motion For Class Certification. Appearances required. The matter will be taken under submission at argument. The parties are reminded that individual issues as such are not fatal to certification, and are asked to comment on how the court should weigh the predominance criteria in this case. The parties are invited to comment on these questions: Do plaintiffs contend that on liability at trial they need establish only that (1) there is a general policy requiring waiver of rest and meal breaks and (2) that violates the law? Do plaintiffs lose on the merits if there is no general policy, but rather show only isolated instances of a compelled waiver? Do plaintiffs need to show that the clerks were compelled to waive (as a condition of employment), and is it thus a defense if clerks voluntarily (willing) consented to the waiver? What is the impact of the fact that in some cases (arguably) work conditions do not allow a meal break: (a) is that irrelevant, not a defense, and simply defendant's problem to fix (by obtaining other staff to cover the break) or (b) may such conditions legally authorize defendant to withhold the break and thus present an issue for trial, presumably creating an individual issue? Specifically what kind of evidence will plaintiffs use to establish that for each class (or sub class) members there were no meal breaks and no rest breaks? Does defendant contend each defendant must testify? If so, specifically on which issue? =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: CHINESE HOSPITAL ASSOCIATION, A CALIFORNIA VS. CHINESE MERCHANTS REALTY ASSOCIATION AKA SEE YIP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Pltf And Pltf'S Counsel
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 2, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 1. DEFENDANT CHINESE MERCHANTS REALTY ASSOCIATION'S Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And Plaintiff's Counsel. Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: TARGET NATIONAL BANK VS. EH SAKULTANAPANICH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Estella Sakultanapanich'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories From Target National Bank, For Sanctions, And In The Alternative, For Preclusion
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 2, 2012, Line 14, Defendant Estella Sakultanapanich'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories From Target National Bank, For Sanctions, And In The Alternative, For Preclusion. To be heard at 9:00 am on the Discovery calendar. Although Ms. Sakultanapanich did not stipulate to the Judge Pro Tem at the hearing on July 26, 2012, the Judge Pro Tem had issued his tentative ruling based solely on the procedural ground that Ms. Sakultanapanich had not provided courtesy copies of her motion per Local Rule 2.6B. At the hearing, Ms. Sakultanapanich presented courtesy copies of her motion and reply. The matter shall now be heard by a Judge Pro Tem on the merits. =(302/CK)
